title: DNA Extraction from Symbiodinium Cultures
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"79uj2","text":"Dinoflagellates are unicellular algae that can have photosynthetic or nonphotosynthetic lifestyles. Dinoflagellates in the genus Symbiodinium can enter endosymbiotic associations with corals, providing the metabolic basis for the highly productive and biologically diverse coral-reef ecosystems (Hoegh-Guldberg, 1999), as well as with other cnidarians, including sea anemones and jellyfish, and noncnidarian hosts (Trench, 1993; Lobban et al., 2002; Mordret et al., 2016).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":129,"length":12},{"style":"italic","offset":436,"length":6},{"style":"italic","offset":458,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"djg2","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"47ssa","text":"Here I describe a protocol for isolating total DNA from Symbiodinium cells.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":56,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

Warm up the buffer in advance at 37ºC into clear liquid if it was precipiated. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Grow Symbiodinium cells in IMK or IMK+casein hydrolysate medium to mid log phase, the concentration is about 10 6 cells/mL.
Spin down the cells at 1000 g at room temperature for 5 minutes, and remove the supernatant.

Warm up the 2X lysis buffer if precipitated. Dilute the buffer using equal volume of water, and make it into 1X lysis buffer. Approximately 500 µl of lysis buffer is needed for 1 million cells. Prepare the appropriate amount of lysis buffer based on the numer of samples for processing.
Add the 500 µl 1X lysis buffer to the cell pellet;
Add 500 µl Phenol:Chloroform:Isoamyl alcohol (25:24:1) to the lysis buffer, and mix well by inverting for a few times.
Separate phases by centrifugation at 13,000 g for 5 minutes, and transfer the upper layer which contains the DNA to a new 1.5 mL eppendorf tube.
Treat the supernatant with 4 µl Ribonuclease A (20mg/mL), and incubate the solution for 30 minutes at 37°C.

Add 500 µl Phenol:Chloroform:Isoamyl alcohol (25:24:1) to the solution and mix well by inverting for a few times; Make sure get clean upper layer from this extraction, do not touch the middle layer. Extract withChloroform:Isoamyl alcohol (24:1) for additional times if needed to fully get rid of proteins.
Add 2.5 volumes of absolute Ethanol, incubate on ice for 30 min or at -20 ºC overnight;
Vortex the pellet to resuspend it well at room temperature.
Tranfer the upper layer phase to a new 1.5 mL eppendorf tube.
Separate phases by centrifugation at 13,000 g at room temperature for 5 min;
Add 500 µl Chloroform:Isoamyl alcohol (24:1) to the solution and mix well by inverting a few times;
Transfer the upper layer phase to a new 1.5 mL eppendorf tube;
Separate phases by centrifugation at 13,000 g at room temperature for 5 min;
Centrifuge the solution at 13,000 g at room temperature for 20 minutes;
Wash the pellet once with 1mL of 70% Ethanol;
Dry the pellet and resupsend in 50 µl H 2 O;
Quantify the DNA concentration by NanoDrop, and run a small amount (for example 1 µl) on 1% agrose gel. Agarose gel electrophoresis of Symbiodinium genomic DNA obtained using this protocol.
creator: Tingting Xiang
